content.problemStatement:
A `union` in C allows you to store different data types in the same memory location. Create a program that utilizes a `union` to store an integer, a float, and a character. The program should then display the value currently stored in the `union` based on user input specifying the data type.


Input Format
The first line contains a character representing the data type to be stored in the union:



`i` for integer
`f` for float
`c` for character

The second line contains the value to be stored. The data type of this value should match the data type specified in the first line.



Output Format
The program should output the value currently stored in the union, formatted according to the specified data type.
constraints: If type is 'i', the integer fits in a 32‑bit signed int.
If type is 'f', the float fits in a standard 32‑bit float.
If type is 'c', the line contains a single printable character.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains a character indicating the data type to store in the union ('i' for integer, 'f' for float, 'c' for character). The second line contains a value of that type.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the value currently stored in the union, formatted according to the specified data type, each on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Three test cases are processed. The first stores integer 10 and prints 10. The second stores float 3.14 and prints 3.14. The third stores character 'H' and prints H.
First case prints the character '*'. Second case prints the integer -7.
Float 0.0 is printed; default formatting removes trailing zeros, resulting in "0".

solution.approach: The problem is a direct simulation. For each test case we read the type character, then read the corresponding value. Because a union shares the same memory for all members, only the value that was last written matters, which is exactly the value we just read. We simply print the value using the appropriate format: %d for integers, %g for floats (which removes unnecessary trailing zeros and uses scientific notation when needed), and %c for characters. The algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.
